summary: Package hijacks global console on import to exfiltrate logs to attacker-controlled Telegram bot
details: On require of this package, `index.js` immediately loads `66o.js`, which replaces the host process's global `console` object with a Proxy. Every subsequent `console.log/error/dir/...` call is POSTed to a hardcoded Telegram Bot API endpoint (`api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage`) into attacker-owned chat IDs, and `console.dir` output is PUT to `https://iiilll.firebaseio.com/<ts>.json`. This is a covert one-way exfiltration channel activated automatically at import time — installer log output frequently contains secrets, request bodies, tokens, and PII. Additionally, `index.js` installs `globalThis.E` helpers that PUT arbitrary payloads to `i----i.firebaseio.com`, upload images to imgbb using a hardcoded third-party API key (`af7cad64d90d19e2a26889f92f6b3ed8`), and relay Telegram update metadata to Cloudinary (`api.cloudinary.com/v1_1/o6/...upload_preset=o6oooo`). The package has a generic name (`8oo`), no README, and no documented legitimate purpose. The combination of auto-executing global console hijack plus multiple hardcoded exfiltration endpoints is unambiguous supply-chain malware.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
